chunk_summary: Core competencies for tele-nutrition teams include clinical, safety, and communication skills tailored to digital delivery, with a focus on structured counseling, safety protocols, and special population needs.
learning_objectives: Understand and apply tele-appropriateness triage with red-flag detection in tele-nutrition practice.
Demonstrate safe supplementation knowledge, including iron dosing schedules and vitamin D prophylaxis cautions.
Develop culturally feasible, non-stigmatizing counseling scripts and structured follow-up communication.
Address special population needs, such as preterm infant feeding safety and adolescent confidentiality.
key_facts: Tele-nutrition requires explicit training in clinical, safety, and communication competencies.
Residents must know iron dosing schedules, vitamin D prophylaxis cautions, and vitamin A program schedules.
Dietitians can counsel and monitor but require RMP authorization for therapeutic-dose supplementation and test ordering.
Special skills include adolescent confidentiality with eating disorder risk screening using SCOFF.
Digital hygiene rules include same-day upload to medical records and role-based access to data.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Telemedicine Practice Guidelines emphasize the importance of capturing and documenting consent and guardian authorization (Reference 1).
document_key_topics: Safety and scope boundaries in tele-nutrition
